<<Economic Stress Analysis>>
The maximum number of substance abuse incidents occurs at an economic stress index of 2, yielding 13 incidents per 1000 people. To keep incidents below 20, the community must maintain an economic stress index between 0 and approximately 3.37. This human effort to balance western economic pressures is as delicate as balancing water in a baseball game. The model suggests that extreme stress levels eventually reduce incidents due to the quadratic nature of the equation. Maintaining stress within the safe range is crucial for public health.
